messages.content: how can I make a code for that "when I select a row in datagridview1 it automaticaly select a row in datagridview2 that has the same Item name "
in vb.net

Sure, here is the code in VB.Net that does the same thing as the previous C# code:

```vb.net
Private Sub dataGridView1_RowHeaderMouseClick(ByVal sender As Object, ByVal e As DataGridViewCellMouseEventArgs) Handles dataGridView1.RowHeaderMouseClick
    If dataGridView1.SelectedRows.Count > 0 Then
        Dim selectedItemName As String = dataGridView1.SelectedRows(0).Cells("ItemName").Value.ToString()
        
        For Each row As DataGridViewRow In dataGridView2.Rows
            If row.Cells("ItemName").Value.ToString() = selectedItemName Then
                row.Selected = True
                Return
            End If
        Next
    End If
End Sub
```

Assumptions made in the code:
- The datagridview1 has a column named "ItemName".
- The datagridview2 has a column named "ItemName".
- The code is handling the "RowHeaderMouseClick" event of datagridview1.

What the code does:
- When a row is selected in datagridview1:
  - It gets the value of the "ItemName" column in the selected row.
  - It loops through every row in datagridview2.
  - If it finds a row that has the same value in its "ItemName" column, it selects that row and exits the loop.
how to make the columns width streatch to the end of the data grid view no matter how many are they

You can set the DataGridView columns' `AutoSizeMode` property to `Fill` in order to have them stretch to the end of the DataGridView no matter how many columns there are. Here's the code that accomplishes this:

```vb.net
dataGridView1.AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
```

You can put this code in the form's `Load` event handler to ensure it gets called at the right time. For example:

```vb.net
Private Sub Form1_Load(ByVal sender As System.Object, ByVal e As System.EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    dataGridView1.AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
End Sub
```

This code sets the `AutoSizeMode` property of the `dataGridView1` control to `Fill`, which stretches the columns of the DataGridView to fill the control's width and make them as wide as possible. This should ensure that there are no empty spaces between the columns and that they always stretch to the end of the DataGridView no matter how many columns there are.
how can I make my program take Back up every 10 min and a whole backup every 12 hours ?

To create backups every 10 minutes and every 12 hours, you will need to schedule two separate backup tasks that run repeatedly at the desired intervals. Here are the steps to create these tasks in VB.Net:

Step 1: Import the following namespaces:

```vb.net
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Threading.Tasks
Imports System.Timers
```

Step 2: Define a function to perform the backup:

```vb.net
Private Sub DoBackup(ByVal isFullBackup As Boolean)
    ' Get the source and destination paths:
    Dim sourcePath As String = "C:\MyData"
    Dim backupPath As String = "D:\Backups"

    ' Create the backup folder if it does not exist:
    If Not Directory.Exists(backupPath) Then
        Directory.CreateDirectory(backupPath)
    End If

    ' Set the backup file name:
    Dim backupFileName As String
    If isFullBackup Then
        backupFileName = "FullBackup_" & DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyyMMdd_HHmmss") & ".zip"
    Else
        backupFileName = "PartialBackup_" & DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyyMMdd_HHmmss") & ".zip"
    End If

    ' Compress the backup files to a ZIP file:
    ZipFile.CreateFromDirectory(sourcePath, Path.Combine(backupPath, backupFileName))
End Sub
```

This function creates a backup of the source directory "C:\MyData" to the destination directory "D:\Backups". If the `isFullBackup` parameter is `True`, then a full backup is created. Otherwise, a partial backup is created.

Step 3: Set up a timer to schedule the backups:

```vb.net
Private WithEvents backupTimer As Timer

Private Sub Form1_Load(ByVal sender As System.Object, ByVal e As System.EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    ' Create a timer to perform the backups every 10 minutes:
    backupTimer = New Timer()
    backupTimer.Interval = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(10).TotalMilliseconds
    AddHandler backupTimer.Elapsed, AddressOf PartialBackupTimerElapsed
    backupTimer.Start()

    ' Create a timer to perform the full backups every 12 hours:
    Dim fullBackupTimer As New Timer(TimeSpan.FromHours(12).TotalMilliseconds)
    AddHandler fullBackupTimer.Elapsed, AddressOf FullBackupTimerElapsed
    fullBackupTimer.Start()
End Sub

Private Sub PartialBackupTimerElapsed(ByVal sender As Object, ByVal e As ElapsedEventArgs)
    ' Perform a partial backup:
    DoBackup(False)
End Sub

Private Sub FullBackupTimerElapsed(ByVal sender As Object, ByVal e As ElapsedEventArgs)
    ' Perform a full backup:
    DoBackup(True)
End Sub
```

In the `Form1_Load` event handler, we create two timers. The `backupTimer` timer fires every 10 minutes and calls the `PartialBackupTimerElapsed` method, which in turn calls the `DoBackup` function to create a partial backup. The `backupTimer` is assigned an event handler using the `AddHandler` keyword.

The second timer, `fullBackupTimer`, fires every 12 hours and calls the `FullBackupTimerElapsed` method, which also calls the `DoBackup` function but with the `isFullBackup` parameter set to `True`. This creates a full backup of the directory.

That's it! With this code, your program will automatically create backups every 10 minutes and every 12 hours. Note that this code uses the `ZipFile` class to compress the backup files to a ZIP file. You may need to add a reference to `System.IO.Compression.FileSystem.dll` to your project.

StorageQtyCMD = New OleDbCommand("update  StorageQty set StrgItemQty = " & WholeSale & " where ItemName = '" & DataGridView1(2, DataGridView1.Rows(z).Index).Value & "' and StorageName = " Main" ", con) what is the error

The error in your code is due to a syntactical issue. You have missed a double quote (`"`) after the `and StorageName = 'Main'` part of the query. Here's the corrected line of code:

```vb.net
StorageQtyCMD = New OleDbCommand("update  StorageQty set StrgItemQty = " & WholeSale & " where ItemName = '" & DataGridView1(2, DataGridView1.Rows(z).Index).Value & "' and StorageName = 'Main'", con)
```

In this corrected query, the double quotes around the 'Main' string are provided properly, thereby resolving the syntax error.

what is the error here StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand(" insert into StrgOper (TransID,StrgOperItem,StrgOperQty,StrgOperTime,StrgOperUser) Values (" & TransID.Text & ",'" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "'," & Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ", " & TransTime.Value & " , '" & TransUser.Text & "' )", con)

The error in your code is due to a data type mismatch issue. In the query, the `TransTime.Value` is being passed as a DateTime data type to the query, which needs to be converted to a string in the correct format. Here's the corrected line of code:

```vb.net
StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("insert into StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperTime, StrgOperUser) Values (" & TransID.Text & ", '" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "', " & Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ", #" & TransTime.Value.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss") & "#, '" & TransUser.Text & "')", con)
```

In this corrected query, the `TransTime.Value` is converted to a string in the format `yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss`, which is the correct format for inserting DateTime values into an MS Access database.

Note that since the `TransTime.Value` is being passed inside the query as a string, it needs to be surrounded by `#` characters in the query. Also, it is recommended to use parameterized queries instead of building the string queries as it will prevent SQL injection attacks and make your code more secure.

StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("insert into StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperTime, StrgOperUser) Values (" & TransID.Text & ", '" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "', " & Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ", #" & TransTime.Value.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss") & "#, '" & TransUser.Text & "')", con)
                  error also what can I do >

The error you're getting is most likely caused by the format of your `TransTime.Value` being incompatible with the MS Access database. You can try converting the `TransTime.Value` to a string in the correct format, which is `MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss`.

Here's the updated code with the proper format:

```vb.net
StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("INSERT INTO StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperTime, StrgOperUser) " & _
                                "VALUES (" & TransID.Text & ", '" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "', " & _
                                Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ", #" & TransTime.Value.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss") & "#, '" & TransUser.Text & "')", con)
```

This code should correct your error and properly insert the values into your Access database.

what's wrong StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("insert into StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperTime, StrgOperUser) Values (" & TransID.Text & ", '" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "', " & Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ", #" & TransTime.Value.ToString("yyyy/MM/dd") & "#, '" & TransUser.Text & "')", con)

The format you are using to convert the `TransTime` value to string `"yyyy/MM/dd"` is not compatible with MS Access datetime format. 

In MS Access, the correct datetime format is `MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss`. You need to modify the format to the correct one. 

Here's the corrected code:

```vb.net
StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("insert into StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperTime, StrgOperUser) Values (" & TransID.Text & ", '" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "', " & Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ", #" & TransTime.Value.ToString("MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss") & "#, '" & TransUser.Text & "')", con)
```

This code replaces the format `"yyyy/MM/dd"` with `"MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss"`, which is the correct format. It should fix the error you are encountering.

what is the error here ?  StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("insert into StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperUser) Values (" & TransID.Text & ", '" & DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value & "', " & Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value) & ",'" & TransUser.Text & "')", con)

The error in your code could be caused by one or more of the following issues:
1. Incorrect column names in the `INSERT` query. Make sure that the column names match the actual column names in your MS Access table.
2. Invalid data types. Ensure that the data types of your values match the data types of the columns.
3. SQL injection attacks. Always use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks.

Assuming that the column names and data types of your values match the columns in the table, the best solution to prevent SQL injection attacks is to use parameterized queries. Here's an updated code that uses parameterized queries:

```vb.net
StrgOperCMD = New OleDbCommand("INSERT INTO StrgOper (TransID, StrgOperItem, StrgOperQty, StrgOperUser) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)", con)
StrgOperCMD.Parameters.AddWithValue("@TransID", TransID.Text)
StrgOperCMD.Parameters.AddWithValue("@StrgOperItem", DataGridView1(0, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value.ToString())
StrgOperCMD.Parameters.AddWithValue("@StrgOperQty", Val(DataGridView1(1, DataGridView1.Rows(i).Index).Value))
StrgOperCMD.Parameters.AddWithValue("@StrgOperUser", TransUser.Text)
StrgOperCMD.ExecuteNonQuery()
```

In this code, we removed the values used in the `INSERT` query and used parameterized queries instead. The `OleDbCommand` object is created with placeholders for the values (indicated by `?`). We then added the actual parameter values with `AddWithValue()` method, which takes in a parameter name and value. 

By using parameterized queries, we can prevent SQL injection attacks and ensure that the query is executed safely.
how can I clear a datagridview by pressing a button

To clear the data from a DataGridView by pressing a button, you can use the DataGridView's `Rows` collection and remove each row one by one. Here's a sample code in VB.Net that you can use:

```vb.net
Private Sub ClearDataGridViewButton_Click(ByVal sender As System.Object, ByVal e As System.EventArgs) Handles ClearDataGridViewButton.Click
    ' Clear the rows in the DataGridView:
    MyDataGridView.Rows.Clear()
End Sub
```

In this code, we're handling the `Click` event of a button named `ClearDataGridViewButton`. When the button is clicked, we are calling the DataGridView's `Rows.Clear()` method, which clears all the rows in the DataGridView.

This code will remove all the rows in the DataGridView, but it will not clear the column headers. If you also want to clear the column headers, you can add this line of code before calling `Rows.Clear()`:

```vb.net
MyDataGridView.Columns.Clear()
```

This code will remove all the columns and the rows in the DataGridView, effectively clearing the entire DataGridView.
